idea.hook: Thinking of visiting the Milky Way's supermassive black hole?
facts: Sagittarius A* (Sgr A*) is 26,000 light-years away at our galaxy's center.
Traveling at the speed of light, it would take 26,000 years to reach Sgr A*.
Our fastest long-distance spacecraft, Voyager 1, cruises at 61,000 km/h (38,000 mph).
idea.payoff: At Voyager 1's speed, reaching Sgr A* would take over 460,000,000 years — far longer than humanity has existed!
idea.title: Road Trip to Our Galaxy's BLACK HOLE! 😱🌌

scenes.text: Thinking of visiting the Milky Way's supermassive black hole?
scenes.visual: Hypnotic accretion disk of a black hole, slowly swirling, surrounded by stars.

scenes.text: Sagittarius A* (Sgr A*) is 26,000 light-years away at our galaxy's center.
scenes.visual: Animated 3D model of the Milky Way galaxy, with a glowing core and an arc highlighting the 26,000 light-year distance.

scenes.text: Traveling at the speed of light, it would take 26,000 years to reach Sgr A*.
scenes.visual: A stylized light beam streaking across deep space, with numbers "26,000 YEARS" quickly animating.

scenes.text: Our fastest long-distance spacecraft, Voyager 1, cruises at 61,000 km/h (38,000 mph).
scenes.visual: A detailed 3D model of the Voyager 1 spacecraft, slowly rotating against a backdrop of distant stars.

scenes.text: At Voyager 1's speed, reaching Sgr A* would take over 460,000,000 years — far longer than humanity has existed!
scenes.visual: An ominous, rapidly expanding black hole visual, with a large counter showing "460,000,000+ YEARS" dramatically.
